position_tiers.conclusion: Current risk schedule only; it cannot identify how much open interest sits near a tier boundary or reconstruct past schedules.

instrument_rules.conclusion: The current instrument record includes listing time but not point-in-time histories of rule, leverage, tick, lot or position-limit changes.

interest_rate_and_loan_quota.conclusion: Current currency-level loan rates and quotas do not reveal historical utilization or a direct perpetual-contract payer.

collateral_discount_schedule.conclusion: Current collateral haircut schedules cannot be retroactively applied to past returns and do not reveal actual collateral holdings.

index_components.conclusion: The current index basket is explicitly cross-venue and has no historical component-weight route in the audited boundary.

premium_history.family_identity: perpetual premium, mark-index and funding convergence
premium_history.conclusion: History exists, but the signal is not independent of FP07 mark-index deviation and FP11 funding carry/crowding.

ideas.mechanism: Positions close to a higher maintenance-margin tier may be forced to reduce risk when volatility rises or the tier boundary is crossed.
ideas.potential_payer: large leveraged accounts near a maintenance-margin boundary
ideas.direction_candidate: trade in the direction of forced position reduction
data_failures: only the current tier schedule is observable
the distribution of account positions across tiers is unobservable
historical tier schedules and rule changes are unavailable
mechanism_failures: the side of constrained positions is not observable
a tier crossing can cause reduction, added collateral or hedging
direction and timing cannot be fixed before returns

ideas.name_cn: 抵押品折扣与折价压力
ideas.mechanism: A lower collateral discount rate or higher liquidation penalty may make a token less useful as margin and induce balance-sheet pressure.
ideas.potential_payer: accounts relying on the asset as collateral
ideas.direction_candidate: short assets facing tighter collateral treatment
data_failures: only the current discount schedule is observable
actual user collateral balances are not public
historical point-in-time discount schedules are unavailable
mechanism_failures: current haircuts may reflect risk rather than cause future returns
collateral users can substitute assets instead of trading the perpetual
the forced-flow amount and horizon are not observable

ideas.name_cn: 借贷额度与利率约束压力
ideas.mechanism: High borrowing costs or a tight loan quota can constrain leveraged demand and require position reduction.
ideas.potential_payer: borrowers consuming scarce stablecoin or crypto credit
ideas.direction_candidate: follow capital scarcity or fade crowded borrowers
data_failures: public quota and rate fields are current snapshots without utilization history
the separate margin-loan-ratio route exposes only 180 daily rows and 179 days
spot-margin borrowing does not identify a perpetual hedge leg
mechanism_failures: continuation and reversal interpretations are both plausible
borrowing the coin can support shorting while borrowing stablecoins can support buying
no unique perpetual direction exists

ideas.mechanism: A dealer internalizes customer flow within an inventory band and hedges externally after inventory exceeds the band.
ideas.potential_payer: liquidity providers forced to externalize adverse inventory
data_failures: dealer inventory is not public
public trades do not identify dealer versus customer
hedge linkage across spot, options and perpetual legs is unavailable
mechanism_failures: the same aggressive trade can be customer information or dealer hedging
internalization can delay or reverse the apparent direction
continuous L2 or identified flow would be required

ideas.name_cn: 资金费感知的库存再平衡
ideas.mechanism: Market makers adjust inventory because holding a perpetual position incurs both mark-to-market risk and state-dependent funding cash flows.
ideas.potential_payer: liquidity providers carrying inventory against an adverse funding state
ideas.direction_candidate: anticipate inventory reduction when funding and inventory costs align
data_failures: market-maker inventory sign and magnitude are not observable
public positioning ratios do not identify market makers
the available observable state collapses back to funding and price premium
mechanism_failures: funding-aware optimization is a market-making control problem, not a unique directional Alpha
without inventory sign, the hedge direction is not identifiable

ideas.mechanism: Changes in index composition or constituent reliability may force hedge or replication adjustments around the perpetual index.
ideas.potential_payer: index replicators and arbitrageurs rebalancing hedge inventories
ideas.direction_candidate: trade the direction implied by constituent weight changes
data_failures: only current component weights and prices are observable
historical point-in-time component weights are unavailable
the index explicitly depends on multiple external venues
mechanism_failures: component rebalancing is an event rather than a recurring direct contract signal
hedge direction depends on the old and new weights and existing inventory

ideas.name_cn: 交易粒度与规则摩擦补偿
ideas.mechanism: Coarse tick size, lot size or minimum order size can change spreads, depth and execution costs.
ideas.potential_payer: participants forced to cross coarse price and quantity grids
ideas.direction_candidate: earn a risk premium from coarse-granularity contracts
data_failures: only current instrument rules are observable
historical tick, lot and minimum-size changes are unavailable
continuous order-book depth is unavailable
mechanism_failures: microstructure effects do not determine a unique long or short return direction
a coarse tick can improve displayed depth while increasing spread costs

ideas.name_cn: 平台持仓上限饱和压力
ideas.mechanism: Approaching an exchange-level position or open-interest limit may cap new risk and create asymmetric closing pressure.
ideas.potential_payer: accounts or market makers unable to add exposure near platform limits
ideas.direction_candidate: trade in the direction of net closing pressure
data_failures: current max platform OI and position-limit fields are often blank or current-only
historical limits are unavailable
actual utilization relative to the limits is unavailable
mechanism_failures: the side of saturated demand is not observable
participants can shift collateral, accounts or hedge venues
the event is not a stable recurring signal

ideas.mechanism: The premium index and funding mechanism pull the perpetual toward its reference index and can affect inventory carrying costs.
ideas.potential_payer: leveraged traders holding the expensive side of the perpetual premium
ideas.direction_candidate: trade convergence or funding-induced inventory adjustment
data_failures: premium history is observable and paginates, but no new independent field is introduced
mechanism_failures: the mechanism is exactly the existing perpetual premium, mark-index and funding family
convergence and crowding reversal variants were already closed

primary_evidence.title: Liquidation, Leverage and Optimal Margin in Bitcoin Futures Markets
primary_evidence.finding: Margin and liquidation constraints are economically real, but the study estimates liquidation risk and optimal margins rather than a unique cross-sectional return direction from public risk tiers.

primary_evidence.title: Algorithmic market making in dealer markets with hedging and market impact
primary_evidence.finding: Dealers may internalize flow inside an inventory band and externalize risk outside it; implementation requires observing dealer inventory or identified hedging flow, neither of which is available.

primary_evidence.title: Funding-Aware Optimal Market Making for Perpetual DEXs
primary_evidence.finding: Funding affects optimal inventory control, but the signal requires the market maker's own inventory state and is not a standalone public directional Alpha.

primary_evidence.title: Fundamentals of Perpetual Futures
primary_evidence.finding: Perpetual premium and funding convergence have a theoretical basis, but they belong to the already closed spot-perpetual, mark-index and funding families.

primary_evidence.title: Tick size and price diffusion
primary_evidence.finding: Tick size affects microstructure and return distributions, but it does not supply a unique directional premium and requires point-in-time rule histories for causal testing.

primary_evidence.title: Implied ETF Carry Rates and the Limits of Arbitrage in Segmented Bitcoin Markets
primary_evidence.finding: Collateral and margin segmentation can sustain carry wedges across products, but the evidence is cross-market and requires external ETF, option and CME fields outside the local OKX-only boundary.

prohibited_rescue_actions: apply current risk tiers, collateral haircuts, loan quotas or instrument rules retroactively to historical returns
infer dealer inventory from OHLCV or aggregate public trades
use current index component weights as if they existed historically
reopen premium, funding or mark-index convergence under a contract-rule name
use rare rule-change, ADL, insurance or liquidation events as a recurring Alpha
open PnL to decide whether a constraint should imply continuation or reversal
allowed_next_work: continue H22, V357 and H27 prospective evidence independently
continue literature and official-field intake for genuinely new recurring OKX contract-level fields
treat risk tiers, collateral schedules, loan quotas and instrument rules as diagnostics until point-in-time histories and actual utilization become available
do not implement or backtest any T13 idea
